title: digitalis × media
intro: Digitalis × media, also known as Foxglove, is a biennial plant species in the figwort family (Scrophulariaceae) that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant contains cardiac glycosides, specifically digoxin and digitoxin, which have been used for centuries to treat various heart conditions and are still used today in the treatment of atrial fibrillation and other heart rhythm disorders. The medicinal properties of Digitalis × media have been found to have health benefits such as reducing heart rate, increasing the force of heart contractions, and improving symptoms of heart failure. Herbal preparations of Digitalis × media include tinctures, teas, and extracts, which are typically used under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to the plant's potential toxicity and interactions with other medications.
